feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.title: The Uplift: Ellie the elephant
entry.content: Meet Big Ellie, the elephant mascot of the WNBA's New York Liberty. Plus more heartwarming news.

entry.title: What does the Golden Knights' 'unsung hero' have in store for Game 3 of the NHL Stanley Cup Final?
entry.content: Brett Howden has become the ‘unsung hero’ for the Vegas Golden Knights during the 2025-26 Stanley Cup playoffs, and with a big Game 3 of the NHL Stanley Cup Final, the Golden Knights will be turning to him again for more offense.

entry.title: Brandon Marsh makes personal Phillies home run history that's hard to believe
entry.content: This is a wild achievement for Marsh in his Phillies career.

entry.title: Why Dodgers' Shohei Ohtani is going to have a tough time winning NL Cy Young award
entry.content: The Dodgers superstar won't have an easy time winning this coveted award.

entry.title: I’m 60 and want a long-term care policy before I retire. Is this a good idea, and how can I find the best option?
entry.content: I’m 60 and want a long-term care policy before I retire. Is this a good idea, and how can I find the best option?

entry.title: Padres' Manny Machado shares strong critique of analytics: 'Too many stats'
entry.content: This is not exactly what Padres fans will want to hear about their struggling star.

entry.title: Revenge of the AI bubble
entry.content: Revenge of the AI bubble

entry.title: Victor Wembanyama called out after finals heartbreaker as NBA legend claims he is being brutally ‘kicked’ around
entry.content: Victor Wembanyama gets a brutal jab from an NBA legend as the Spurs came up just short in a 105-104 loss to the Knicks.

entry.title: Victor Wembanyama’s aggression flip changes everything after one fiery locker room talk revealed following Game 2 loss
entry.content: Despite Victor Wembanyama's late mistake, the Spurs, including guard De’Aaron Fox, continued to show confidence in him.

entry.title: Lil Yachty set for Belmont Stakes as Vitruvian Man takes Triple Crown shot
entry.content: Vitruvian Man is the longest shot in Saturday's field at 30-1, but Lil Yachty is flying to Saratoga anyway to watch the colt he backs with Lil Wayne run.

entry.title: Two things that left Spurs frozen in final 30 seconds vs Knicks could cost them entire series
entry.content: Jeremy Lin explains why Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs trail the Knicks 2-0 after a brutal Game 2 NBA Finals collapse.

entry.title: Dow Jones Futures: What To Do As Market Rally Faces First Real Test; SpaceX IPO, Apple, Oracle Loom
entry.content: Dow Jones Futures: What To Do As Market Rally Faces First Real Test; SpaceX IPO, Apple, Oracle Loom

entry.title: Trump issued pardon to former Republican congressman convicted of insider trading
entry.content: Trump issued pardon to former Republican congressman convicted of insider trading

entry.title: The next NVIDIA? These 3 stocks below $50 are poised to win the next AI boom (and they’re still flying under the radar)
entry.content: The next NVIDIA? These 3 stocks below $50 are poised to win the next AI boom (and they’re still flying under the radar)

entry.title: Eli Lilly Leads Medical Stocks To Watch, While Google Finds Support
entry.content: Eli Lilly Leads Medical Stocks To Watch, While Google Finds Support

entry.title: Lawmakers invest in tech, AI, and crypto as new rules for the industries are on their agenda
entry.content: Lawmakers invest in tech, AI, and crypto as new rules for the industries are on their agenda

entry.title: Israeli forces kill Palestinian baby in the West Bank: Health ministry
entry.content: Sam Fahd Abu Haikal was killed Friday evening, and his parents were wounded, the Palestinian health ministry said.

entry.title: ‘Historic’: Canadian warehouse workers sign first-ever union deal with Walmart
entry.content: Union says collective agreement is just the start of a broader fight to unionize major employers across the countryCanadian warehouse workers have signed the first-ever collective agreement with Walmart, a breakthrough labour organizers are calling a “historic and powerful step”.But the union says the deal with a corporation long hostile to organized labour is only an opening salvo in a broader fight to unionize major employers across the country. Continue reading...

entry.title: The Era of the One-Size-Fits-All Cancer Drug Is Ending
entry.content: Investors have bet new drugs can topple Merck’s Keytruda, but the evidence so far points to something smaller.

entry.title: Mass Ukrainian Drone Strikes Target Russian Military Facilities in St. Petersburg
entry.content: The attack came a day after Russian President Vladimir Putin gave a speech in the city in which he rejected a call for peace talks from Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky.

entry.title: U.S., Iran Exchange Fire as Hormuz Tensions Persist
entry.content: The skirmish was the latest in a string that has tested the two-month-old ceasefire.

entry.title: Aviation industry looks skywards as leaders fly in for Rio summit
entry.content: Oil tankers may be stuck behind strait of Hormuz, but holding the Iata AGM in Brazil defies warnings of impending shortagesNothing says jet fuel crisis, as one prospective attender put it, like flying everyone to Rio de Janeiro. Aviation leaders will converge in Brazil this weekend for the Iata AGM, the annual global airline summit, with the industry still, for the most part, looking resolutely skyward.The oil tankers may still be stuck behind the strait of Hormuz as the conflict between the US, Israel and Iran flickers on, but for now, airlines continue to defy dire warnings of impending shortages which had stoked fears of a summer of chaos for European holidaymakers. Continue reading...

entry.title: Removing ‘invisibility cloaks’ and safely skipping chemo: new weapons in war on cancer shared at US conference
entry.content: Drug that stops cancer cells hiding and a breakthrough for pancreatic cancer among highlights from Asco conference – but there were also notes of cautionDoctors, scientists and researchers shared new research about ways to tackle cancer at the 2026 American Society of Clinical Oncology (Asco) annual meeting, the world’s largest cancer conference.The event in Chicago, attended by 40,000 health professionals, featured more than 200 sessions and 2,700 poster presentations on this year’s theme, “the science and practice of translation: improving cancer outcomes worldwide”. Here are the five biggest takeaways. Continue reading...

entry.title: Ebola spread in central Africa could match 2014 record outbreak, US health officials say
entry.content: Modelling from US CDC shows Ebola spread could be on ‘dangerous trajectory’, but experts warn outbreaks can be very hard to predictCentral Africa’s Ebola outbreak could spread to be similar in scale to the worst outbreak in history, west Africa’s 2014-2016 outbreak that killed more than 11,000 people, according to a new analysis by US health officials.The US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) on Friday published a range of scenarios generated by computer models, from 10,000 cases to more than 20,000. In the west Africa outbreak, more than 28,000 cases were reported. Continue reading...

entry.title: No bags, no watch parties: What to expect when Trump attends NBA Finals
entry.content: As President Trump prepares to watch the New York Knicks take on the San Antonio Spurs at Madison Square Garden, officials are planning for a heightened security posture, sources said.

entry.title: SoFi Stadium workers authorize strike ahead of 2026 FIFA World Cup in LA
entry.content: Roughly 2,000 SoFi Stadium workers overwhelmingly authorized a strike a week before the 2026 FIFA World Cup kicks off in Los Angeles.

entry.title: ‘Maine had my back’: Graham Platner hits the campaign trail amid latest turmoil
entry.content: Hundreds of supporters signaled they weren’t phased by the new allegations about Graham Platner’s past relationships, giving the Democratic Senate candidate a warm welcome on Friday.

entry.title: Putin’s Inner Circle Travels on Western-Made Private Jets Despite Sanctions
entry.content: Russia’s elite have been forced to adapt since the start of the war, but sanctions haven’t done much to crimp their globe-spanning lifestyles.

entry.title: Graham Platner faces another controversy days ahead of Maine Senate primary
entry.content: This week, the New York Times reported allegations of Platner's "unsettling" behavior toward women he dated, including one claim that he was physically abusive, which Platner denies.

entry.title: U.S. and Iran trade another round of fire
entry.content: It's unclear how close the U.S. and Iran are to striking a deal, as an Iranian official says "the ball is in Trump's court," and Israel-Hezbollah hostilities continue.

entry.title: Pentagon raised threat of Israeli spying on U.S. to highest level, sources say
entry.content: WASHINGTON — The Pentagon is increasingly concerned about Israel ramping up its spying on the U.S., recently raising the counterintelligence threat level from America’s top ally in the Middle East to the highest level, according to two U.S. officials and one former U.S. official

entry.title: Iran Demands Cash for Peace. That’s a Political Minefield for Trump.
entry.content: Tehran wants quick access to its frozen funds, a major hurdle to a deal with Washington to end the war.

entry.title: Becerra advances in California governor's race, CBS News projects
entry.content: Former U.S. Health and Human Services Secretary Xavier Becerra, a Democrat, will advance to the November election in the California governor's race, CBS News projects. A second candidate in the race has not yet been projected to advance.

entry.title: Colombia's Petro accuses Trump of intervening in his country's election
entry.content: Colombian President Gustavo Petro argues the U.S. has chosen to align against his government and back forces he identifies as complicit in the drug trade.

entry.title: Actor James Handy allegedly killed by girlfriend's son at LA home
entry.content: Michael Gledhill, 44, was arrested on suspicion of murder after he turned himself in following the fatal stabbing of Handy, the LAPD said.

entry.title: Los Angeles police chase ends in fatal shooting of suspect on 405 Freeway
entry.content: The freeways of Los Angeles saw two big police pursuits on Friday. The first chase ended when authorities reported that a robbery suspect was shot and killed on the busy 405 Freeway during morning rush hour. In the second incident, an alleged carjacker was taken down by a police K-9 following a meandering three-hour chase. Carter Evans has more.

entry.title: Ole Miss vs. Auburn baseball box score: Live stats from NCAA Baseball Championship Game
entry.content: The Rebels and Tigers battle in the Auburn Super Regional.

entry.title: Knicks vs Spurs box score: Full stats from NBA Finals Game 2
entry.content: Spurs will try to even the series before heading to New York.

entry.title: Donald Trump addresses sky-high NBA Finals ticket prices: 'They can watch it on television'
entry.content: Donald Trump didn't seem too fussed with the heightened prices present at NBA Finals games at Madison Square Garden.

entry.title: Democrat Xavier Becerra advances to California governor general election, NBC News projects
entry.content: Former Health and Human Services Secretary Xavier Beccera will advance to the general election in the race for governor in California, NBC News projects. NBC News’ Liz Kreutz discuss the latest election results and what impacts the speed of vote counting in the state.

entry.title: Knicks vs. Spurs live score, updates, highlights from 2026 NBA Finals Game 2
entry.content: Will the Knicks go up 2-0, or can the Spurs even the series? The Sporting News is tracking live scoring updates and highlights from Game 2 of the 2026 NBA Finals on Friday night.

entry.title: Why is De'Aaron Fox starting over Dylan Harper? Former All-Star remains in Mitch Johnson's lineup vs. Knicks
entry.content: While the Spurs' star rookie has been terrific in the playoffs, he continues to lead the second unit. Here's why.

entry.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce to reportedly have wedding at Madison Square Garden
entry.content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are rumored to get married this summer.

entry.title: Mexico Is Starting to Push Back Against Trump’s Pressure Campaign
entry.content: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum has refused to extradite an indicted governor, rejecting U.S. measures she says are destabilizing her government.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
You never show your vulnerability, you are always self assured and confident.
Lucky Numbers: 45, 11, 5, 97, 79, 85

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Use hair clips to organize long wires.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: It is impossible to lick your elbow.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Confucius
entry.content: “The man who asks a question is a fool for a minute, the man who does not ask is a fool for life.” — Confucius

entry.title: Super Yooka-Laylee Kart looks like an old-school Mario Kart for the modern age
entry.content: Playtonic is shifting the Yooka-Laylee series from platforming to familiar-looking arcade racing.

entry.title: Little Rock vs. Troy baseball box score: Final stats from NCAA Baseball Championship Game
entry.content: The Trojan War will be a good one in Troy.

entry.title: Party with your grandma and prevent her murder in Apple Crumble, out this year
entry.content: Duck Detective studio Happy Broccoli is back with a creepy-cute mystery called Apple Crumble.

entry.title: Democrat Xavier Becerra advances to general election in California governor’s race
entry.content: Former Health and Human Services Secretary Xavier Becerra has advanced to the general election in the California governor’s race, NBC News projects

entry.title: Latest predictions link Cardinals to SEC catcher with rising power
entry.content: This player could be a great fit for the Cardinals.

entry.title: '5D' platformer Screenbound arrives on September 10
entry.content: Screenbound lets you control a 2D handheld game and a character in a 3D world at the same time.

entry.title: Ithaca is a mysterious road-trip RPG from Bury Me, My Love studio Pixel Hunt
entry.content: Ithaca asks how far will players go to save the planet.

entry.title: Kylie Jenner stuns in micro pink bikini after ditching Game 1 with Timothée Chalamet
entry.content: Timothée Chalamet cheered on the Knicks in San Antonio.

entry.title: How the FBI and law enforcement plan to deploy drones to keep FIFA World Cup games safe
entry.content: The FBI prepares to tackle drone security ahead of FIFA World Cup games spanning across the country. NBC News' Kelly O’Donnell goes behind the scenes with the FBI to see how its own drones and partnerships with local law enforcement will keep the events safe.

entry.title: In co-op shooter Lazy River, peeing in the pool turns guests into zombies
entry.content: Peeing in the pool is extra dangerous in co-op shooter Lazy River.

entry.title: 2026 Nordic Darts Masters: Draw, schedule, prize money and format explained
entry.content: The World Series of Darts returns this weekend after a few months off.

entry.title: N PLUS INFINITY TIMES TWO threatens to revitalize 2D platforming in 2027
entry.content: Metanet is bringing minimalism and precise mechanics to consoles and PC next year.

entry.title: Fifa clarifies water bottle stadium rule
entry.content: Fifa says fans will be able to bring sealed disposable water bottles into World Cup stadiums following criticism over a decision to ban reusable bottles.

entry.title: Tenebris Somnia adds live-action horror to a retro survival game on October 16
entry.content: Airdorf and Andrés Borghi are making one nightmare with two styles of terror.

entry.title: Final Fantasy 7 Revelation will launch across all platforms in Spring 2027
entry.content: The final chapter will drop simultaneously on PS5, Switch 2, Xbox and PC.

entry.title: Trump calls for military to accelerate use of AI while protecting Americans
entry.content: President Donald Trump is calling for the U.S. military and national security agencies to accelerate their use of artificial intelligence

entry.title: Founders share VC horror stories, and some are naming names
entry.content: A massive viral conversation sharing VC horror stories has taken place this week on X. Some are weird. Some are infuriating.

entry.title: Carnage in Chip Stocks Hits Extra Hard in Top-Heavy Market
entry.content: The major stock indexes have been dependent on a small group of big tech companies.

entry.title: Former cyber executive turned whistleblower accuses IBM of covering up several data breaches
entry.content: IBM and two of its subsidiary companies were allegedly breached during the mid-2010s, which a lawsuit filed by a former cybersecurity executive accuses IBM of not disclosing and actively covering up.

entry.title: Strong Job Growth Weakens Stocks
entry.content: Plus, SpaceX may already be grounding other megacap techs

entry.title: Gyllenhaal's 'The Bride!' seeks 4K cult life after box office death
entry.content: "The Bride!" on 4K delivers bold visuals, noir shadows, neon color, Jessie Buckley's energy and a divisive take on Frankenstein lore.

entry.title: Startup Battlefield 200 applications officially close in 3 days
entry.content: Applications for Startup Battlefield 200 officially close on June 8, 11:59 p.m. PT. Don't wait any longer. Secure your shot at competing on the Disrupt Stage at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 this October at San Francisco's Moscone West.

entry.title: National Park Service ranger dies after falling into a crevasse on Alaska's Mount McKinley
entry.content: National Park Service ranger dies after falling into a crevasse on Alaska's Mount McKinley

entry.title: Hot jobs report puts Fed cuts further out of reach as Chair Warsh faces policy tests
entry.content: Another big jobs report in May has swept aside the possibility of interest rate cuts anytime soon.

entry.title: Google will pay SpaceX $920M per month for compute
entry.content: In a statement, a Google representative described the deal as a result of unexpected demand for its recently launched AI products.

entry.title: At Russia’s Davos, Putin Tells His Soldiers to Keep Fighting
entry.content: The Kremlin chief rejected Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky’s offer to meet and appeared more interested in boosting relations with the American right.

entry.title: Former Channel 4 News anchor Jon Snow diagnosed with Alzheimer’s
entry.content: Long-serving presenter talks about diagnosis in documentary on mining disaster to be broadcast on 20 JuneThe former Channel 4 News anchor Jon Snow has been diagnosed with Alzheimer’s disease, the most common form of dementia, the Alzheimer’s Society has said.Snow, who presented his last news bulletin in December 2021, will take part in a documentary that will be broadcast on Channel 4 and in which he talks about his diagnosis. Continue reading...

entry.title: The death throes of glaciers make for an unusually personal doc in 'Time and Water'
entry.content: Filmmaker Sara Dosa ('Fire of Love') continues to mine human intimacy from natural backdrops, settings to which we are more connected than we realize.

entry.title: GM’s electric future depends on a new battery — and this facility
entry.content: GM wants to slash EV prices by deploying new battery tech up to a year earlier than planned. This building is key to making that happen.

entry.title: As VC-backed e-bike startups went bankrupt, bootstrapped Lectric grew
entry.content: Lectric, which says the U.S. market is ripe for competition and choice, has launched three new brands in the past six months.

entry.title: The new 'Scary Movie' is cringey, crass and salvaged only by its devoted cast
entry.content: The sixth installment of the spoof series is actually the best one yet (not a huge compliment), with daffy turns by stars Anna Faris and Regina Hall.

entry.title: Anthony Head, British star of 'Ted Lasso,' 'Merlin' and 'Buffy the Vampire Slayer,' dies at 72
entry.content: British actor Anthony Head, whose decades-long acting career includes roles in 'Ted Lasso' and 'Buffy the Vampire Slayer,' has died. The actor's family announced his death on Friday. He was 72.

entry.title: The most interesting startups right now want to get you off your phone
entry.content: While the AI fundraising machine keeps breaking its own records, some founders are building in the other direction. Mirror founder Brynn Putnam just raised money for Board, a startup focused on bringing people together through in-person games and social experiences. Cyberdeck creators are going viral crafting whimsical DIY computers that literally encourage users to touch grass. Unlike the AI-free browser crowd, this doesn’t just feel like backlash, […]

entry.title: Jury awards $176M for wrongful deaths of young brothers struck by California socialite's car
entry.content: A Los Angeles jury has awarded $176 million for the hit-and-run deaths of two young brothers struck in a crosswalk by a California socialite

entry.title: Connecticut police officer hugs supporters, appears in court in fatal shooting of Black man
entry.content: A white police officer in Connecticut who fatally shot a Black man suffering a mental health crisis has made his first court appearance on a manslaughter charge

entry.title: WGA East and SAG-AFTRA call out the new leaders at CBS News and '60 Minutes'
entry.content: After weeks of turmoil at CBS's legacy newsmagazine '60 Minutes,' WGA East and SAG-AFTRA are slamming the network for its most recent actions, citing a lack of editorial independence.

entry.title: Bill Whitaker, Lesley Stahl and Jon Wertheim will remain at '60 Minutes'
entry.content: "We don't want '60 Minutes' to die, the correspondents said in a joint memo.

entry.title: Planning commission seeks more details on Trump's planned 250-foot arch near the Lincoln Memorial
entry.content: The National Capital Planning Commission has kept alive the triumphal arch that President Trump wants built at an entrance to the nation's capital, requesting more information on how it could potentially impact air travel navigation in the area, as well as other specifics on construction and traffic in the area.

entry.title: Stock Indexes Are Divided on Rules for Megacap IPOs. How Exposed Will You Be?
entry.content: SpaceX isn’t public yet, but Elon Musk’s rocket maker might still be part of your portfolio before the month’s end.

entry.title: SAG-AFTRA members approve deal with major studios
entry.content: SAG-AFTRA says the new four-year deal offers protections related to artificial intelligence, increases minimum wage rates by 3% annually and merges the pension plans of previously separate unions SAG and AFTRA.

entry.title: More than half of Latin Americans deported from US to Congo are now back home
entry.content: More than half of Latin Americans deported from US to Congo are now back home

entry.title: How to prevent older people from having fatal falls | Letters
entry.content: Jules Robinson outlines the targeted support needed to prevent accidental deaths, and Sara Hazzard urges investment in rehabilitation and the physiotherapy workforceDenis Campbell’s article (GPs in England too ‘overloaded’ to help older people at risk of falling, say MPs, 3 June) draws welcome attention to a severe but often overlooked health crisis. Research by the Royal Society for the Prevention of Accidents (RoSPA) shows that falls are the leading cause of accidental death in the UK, killing over 11,000 people a year, more than 9,000 of whom are aged 75 and over. And this crisis is getting worse, with a 12% increase in the rate of deaths over a single year.Falls are preventable, and should not be regarded as just an inevitable part of ageing. The causes are varied and complex, so intervention must take into account a person’s living environment and access to networks of support as well as their physical and mental health. Such a detailed multifactorial assessment requires not just specialist expertise but far more time than is available within a short GP appointment. RoSPA is calling for equitable access to falls and fracture liaison services, removing the variation in treatment available depending on postcodes. Without such targeted support there is a real risk that fatal falls will continue to increase, taking the lives of vulnerable people in tragic accidents that could be prevented.Jules RobinsonRoSPA Continue reading...

entry.title: Test Your Personal Finance Knowledge Against These Savvy High-Schoolers, Part 2
entry.content: Here are more questions from the final round of this year’s National Personal Finance Challenge.

entry.title: NASA orders ISS crew to shelter as Russian air leak worsens
entry.content: NASA ordered four astronauts aboard the International Space Station to don their spacesuits and shelter inside a docked spacecraft Friday as Russian engineers worked to repair a worsening air leak in the station's Russian segment, the agency said.

entry.title: South Africa rolls out game-changing HIV shot amid funding shortfalls
entry.content: A new twice-yearly HIV prevention injection could transform South Africa's fight against the epidemic — but U.S. aid cuts and limited doses threaten to slow its impact.

entry.title: Prosecutor wants to charge 14-year-old as adult after allegedly murdering business school graduate
entry.content: Prosecutor wants to charge 14-year-old as adult after allegedly murdering business school graduate

entry.title: The right’s culture war over prostate cancer screening is damaging trust in medicine | Polly Toynbee
entry.content: The decision not to test all men and only screen the most at risk, including black men, is fact-based. Yet it’s been called ‘two-tier’ – and labelled as misandryIf the country seems to be slipping away from reason and trust in science, blame usually falls on modern phenomena such as social media and its fantastical influencers. Or on the US health secretary Robert F Kennedy Jr’s bizarre anti-vaccine, anti-fluoride, anti-evidence lunacy. But campaigns against the UK national screening committee’s decision to limit prostate cancer testing have been run by British bastions of the sort laying claim to “common sense”. They include two Tory ex-prime ministers, David Cameron and Rishi Sunak (who see themselves as sensibles, unlike Boris Johnson and Liz Truss), joined by their Tory/Reform media, especially the Mail and the Telegraph, plus a host of distinguished campaigners such as Stephen Fry, fount of QI knowledge.The national screening committee (NSC) has for a long time resisted a call for universal testing of all men for prostate cancer, though it kills 12,000 men a year in the UK. I was on the committee in the 1990s, and it was besieged by demands for screening for prostate cancer and numerous other conditions. These were often refused for unreasonable cost, but this decision is about harm to men, not about money.Polly Toynbee is a Guardian columnistDo you have an opinion on the issues raised in this article? If you would like to submit a response of up to 300 words by email to be considered for publication in our letters section, please click here. Continue reading...

entry.title: Ruby Duncan, Who Stormed Caesars Palace to Protest Cuts in Public Aid, Dies at 93
entry.content: An organizer, Duncan was hailed as an advocate for Nevada’s poor and racially oppressed.

entry.title: Alan Jackson's farewell special to be broadcast on NBC/Peacock
entry.content: Alan Jackson's farewell concert on June 29 will be broadcast later as an NBC/Peacock special "Alan Jackson: The Last Show."

entry.title: Woman gunned down in hail of 70 to 80 bullets in mistaken identity killing: Police
entry.content: Woman gunned down in hail of 70 to 80 bullets in mistaken identity killing: Police

entry.title: American Pleads Guilty to Working as Chinese Agent in U.S.
entry.content: Thomas Pauken II is the latest in a series of prosecutions of Americans accused of secretly working for Beijing.
